data.title: CNFinance to implement 1-for-10 reverse ADS split
data.description:
• CNFinance Holdings (NYSE:CNF) will change its ADS ratio from 1 ADS:20 Class A shares to 1 ADS:200 Class A shares.
  • The change effectively represents a 1-for-10 reverse ADS split for holders of ADSs.
  • The change is expected to take effect on or around September 5, 2025.
  • CNF shares down 5.7% post-market on Friday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Westport files preliminary short form base shelf prospectus
data.description:
• Westport Fuel Systems (NASDAQ:WPRT) on Friday announced it has filed a preliminary short form base shelf prospectus with the securities commissions in each of the provinces and territories of Canada to replace its prior base shelf prospectus that expired on June 18, 2025.
  • The shelf prospectus, when made final, will allow Westport to offer up to $100M of common shares, preferred shares, subscription receipts, warrants, debt securities, or units, or any combination thereof, during the 25-month period that the shelf prospectus will be effective.
  • The shelf prospectus will enable Westport to access new capital or issue securities in connection with strategic acquisitions if and when needed.
  • WPRT +0.69% after hours to $2.89.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Home Depot and Lowe's gear up for earnings amid housing and DIY crosswinds
data.description:
The leaders in the home improvement space – Home Depot (NYSE:HD) and Lowe’s (NYSE:LOW) – report second quarter results next week with Wall Street bracing for potential red flags regarding inflation, the housing market, and consumer sentiment, all of which could signal a tough second half for the sector.

After reporting a 9.5% increase in sales with a 0.3% drop in comparable store sales in the first quarter, Home Depot (NYSE:HD) needs to show improved foot traffic, although anything in the low-single-digit range would be considered a positive given the anemic traffic in the first quarter. Wells Fargo analyst Zachary Fadem is expecting another quarter of soft comp sales given the still sluggish housing market and disappointing sales in May and June, although the general consensus among analysts is for a 1.4% jump in comp sales, a dramatic improvement from Q1 contraction. Once again, professional ((Pro)) comparable sales will outperform DIYers at Home Depot (NYSE:HD) with Fadem predicting 1.5% and 0.5% growth, respectively.

On the profit side, modest freight savings and decreasing shrink should help underpin margins although estimates still target a 60 basis point compression in gross margin from the prior quarter.

With Q2 results scheduled before the open on Tuesday, August 19, the Seeking Alpha consensus estimate sets an adjusted profit of $4.70 per share for Home Depot (HD), a 0.6% improvement from the same quarter last year as revenue is expected to increase as much as 14% to $45.4B.

When Lowe’s (NYSE:LOW) reports before the open on Wednesday, August 20, the company should see similar overall improvement in Pro trends based on weekend versus weekday traffic data, while DIY trends improved in May before softening again in June. Wells Fargo anticipates Lowe’s (NYSE:LOW) Q2 traffic was up 90 basis points versus the prior quarter, and up 280 basis points on a 2-year basis.

During the first quarter, Lowe’s realized modest gross margin expansion as COGS were lower year-over-year but missed revenue expectations as comparable sales contracted from the same quarter a year ago.

But for the second quarter, the warmer weather likely translated into increased traffic, nearly reversing the 1.7% decline in comp sales in Q1, while the street is expecting 10 basis points of gross margin compression on supply chain investments. On the plus side, the company could see a tailwind from softer interest rates and easing inflationary pressures on discretionary spending.

Seeking Alpha’s consensus estimate forecasts an adjusted profit of $4.24 per share on $23.95B in sales.

data.title: Youxin Technology receives Nasdaq notices for non-compliance
data.description:
• Youxin Technology (NASDAQ:YAAS) received two determination notices from Nasdaq on August 13, 2025.

  • The notices cite non-compliance with the minimum bid price requirement.

  • The company is also required to maintain a minimum market value of listed securities of $35M.

  • The notices put Youxin Technology at risk of delisting if compliance is not regained.

  • YAAS shares fall 4.5% post-market on Friday. 

  • Source: Press release

data.title: SenesTech files to sell 2.26M shares of common stock by selling shareholders
data.description:
• SenesTech (NASDAQ:SNES) filed to sell 2.26 million shares of common stock.
  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell these securities.
  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of common stock by the selling shareholder.
  • Filing

data.title: Targa Exploration upsizes non-brokered private placement to C$950,000
data.description:
• Targa Exploration (OTCQB:TRGEF) increased its non-brokered private placement to C$950,000 from the previously announced amount.
  • The offering consists of flow-through shares priced at C$0.15 per share.
  • Net proceeds will fund exploration of Targa’s Opinaca gold project in Quebec.
  • Closing is expected on or about August 22, 2025.
  • Source: Press release
